mission.title: Install and Configure Ingress NGINX Controller
mission.description: Ingress NGINX Controller is a Kubernetes Ingress controller that uses NGINX as a reverse proxy and load balancer. It is suitable for managing external access to services in a Kubernetes cluster. However, it is important to note that this project is retiring and will not receive further updates after March 2026.

steps.title: Add the Ingress NGINX Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Ingress NGINX Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Ingress NGINX Controller.

```bash

helm repo add ingress-nginx https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/main/deploy/helm-chart

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Helm repository for Ingress NGINX.
> Updates the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.

steps.title: Install the Ingress NGINX Controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Ingress NGINX Controller using Helm, specifying the desired version and namespace.

```bash

helm install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --version 4.10.1 --namespace ingress-nginx --create-namespace

```

> Installs the Ingress NGINX Controller in the 'ingress-nginx' namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Ingress NGINX Controller pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx

```

> This command lists the pods in the 'ingress-nginx' namespace to verify that they are running.

steps.title: Create an example Ingress resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Ingress resource to demonstrate how to route traffic to a service.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

```

> This creates a sample Ingress resource that routes traffic to 'example-service'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: default
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Ingress resource
steps.description:
This step checks that the Ingress resource is created and ready.

```bash

kubectl get ingress -n default

```

> This command lists the Ingress resources in the default namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Ingress NGINX Controller
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ingress NGINX Controller and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall ingress-nginx --namespace ingress-nginx

kubectl delete namespace ingress-nginx

```

> This command uninstalls the Ingress NGINX Controller.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Ingress NGINX Controller
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up the current configuration, upgrading the controller, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get ingress -n default -o yaml > ingress-backup.yaml

helm upgrade ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx --version 4.10.1 --namespace ingress-nginx

kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx

```

> Backup the current Ingress resources.
> Upgrades the Ingress NGINX Controller to the specified version.
> Verify that the Ingress NGINX Controller pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get ingress -n default -o yaml > ingress-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (InternalError): error when creating "-": Internal error occurred: failed calling webhook "validate.nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io": Post "http://ingress-nginx-controller-admiss
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the admission webhook for the Ingress NGINX Controller is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the ingress-nginx-controller-admission service and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: '503 Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error typically occurs when the backend service is not reachable or not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the backend service specified in the Ingress resource is running and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Ingress" in version "networking.k8s.io/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the Kubernetes cluster version does not support the Ingress resource in the specified API version.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Kubernetes cluster version is 1.19 or higher, or adjust the Ingress resource to use a supported API version.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.18

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create listener: failed to create HTTP listener: listen tcp :80: bind: address already in use'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when another service is already using port 80.

**Fix:**
Check for other services using port 80 and either stop them or configure Ingress NGINX to use a different port.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom error pages and improved logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for legacy Ingress annotations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.12 or earlier, review the new Ingress resource specifications and update your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the Ingress NGINX Controller pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx'.
codeSnippets:

kind: Ingress
metadata:

    http:
      paths:

        pathType: Prefix
        backend:

            port:
              number: 80
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
